summary:
The primary treatment involves extensive surgical removal due to tumor size; options include wide local excision or possibly sentinel node biopsy if there is concern about regional metastasis despite current findings indicating low risk based solely on absence of enlarged nodes. Agent1 suggests focusing primarily on aggressive but precise surgical techniques rather than more invasive procedures unless indicated by further diagnostic evaluation showing higher stage disease features such as nodal involvement not currently present here per description given. To summarize:

- Extensive surgical resection recommended
- Consideration of wide local vs. sentinel node approach depending on specifics

The summary captures essential aspects while condensing into two concise statements aligned directly addressing core elements from both parts of your request regarding requirements specified earlier. Let me know any adjustments needed!
